studies.preview: Liver allograft with patent hepatic artery, portal and hepatic veins; normal flow. NO ascites. Marked interval resolution of the previously large-volume ascites.
Preserved LV systolic function (EF 60%), no regional wall-motion abnormality, no significant valvular disease, normal RV. No primary cardiac explanation for the acute pulmonary edema.
Diffuse BILATERAL alveolar/interstitial pulmonary edema with vascular congestion. Different from her prior unilateral transudative effusion — an acute volume-overload pattern.
Moderate-to-large RIGHT pleural effusion with associated basilar atelectasis. No focal consolidation. Pattern consistent with hepatic hydrothorax. Left lung clear.
Coarse, nodular cirrhotic liver with splenomegaly and large-volume ascites. Patent portal vein (hepatopetal flow). No focal liver lesion. Findings of portal hypertension.
